findings.implication: Plain English Summary of Research: The section clearly outlines the problem, aims, methods, and beneficiaries in accessible language, supporting pr.1.
Plain English Summary of Research: Identifies a knowledge gap regarding GP guidance for PLWHIV and plans a review of recent studies, supporting pr.2.
Plain English Summary of Research: Describes a three-part methodology including literature review, database analysis, and co-design with a steering group, supporting pr.4.
Plain English Summary of Research: Explicitly states the goal to improve healthcare for PLWHIV to help them live longer, healthier lives, supporting pr.5.
Plain English Summary of Research: Details the Person-Based Approach, PPI involvement in proposal writing, steering group co-leadership, and feedback loops, strongly supporting pr.6.
Plain English Summary of Research: Highlights central input from PLWHIV in planning, co-design of the care model, and feedback mechanisms, supporting wp.1.

Plain English Summary of Research: Outlines a dissemination plan including journals, conferences, social media, and press releases to share findings with the public and health workers, supporting wp.3.
Detailed Research Plan: Lists team members' prior experience with NIHR grants, electronic health records, and scoping reviews, aligning skills with project needs.
Detailed Research Plan: Mentions track records of publications and successful NIHR grant applications for key team members, indicating impactful outputs.
Detailed Research Plan: Identifies joint leads and co-applicants as GP academics and HIV specialists, demonstrating a practitioner-academic team structure.
Detailed Research Plan: Defines three workstreams with specific timelines (months 1-16) and methods (scoping review, CPRD analysis, co-design), showing feasible scope.
Detailed Research Plan: Details rigorous methods including Arksey and O'Malley framework for scoping review, CPRD database parameters, and regression analysis.
Detailed Research Plan: Explicitly links research to NHS priorities and aims to produce evidence-based guidance to improve care and resource use for PLWHIV.
Detailed Research Plan: Describes a Person-Based Approach with PLWHIV co-applicants leading a stakeholder group and focus groups for co-design.
Detailed Research Plan: Identifies underrepresented groups (trans, Black African, rural) and plans extra efforts for their representation in focus groups.
Detailed Research Plan: Highlights team members' track records of successful NIHR grants and publications in HIV care, indicating strong institutional capability.
Detailed Research Plan: Assigns specific roles for methodological advice (MTR), PPI training (SD, ANe), and co-leadership of stakeholder groups, showing structured support.
Detailed Research Plan: Integrates PLWHIV as co-applicants and stakeholder group leaders to co-design the intervention, ensuring lived experience shapes the model.
Detailed Research Plan: Explicitly targets men who have sex with men, Black African people, trans individuals, and those in non-metropolitan areas for inclusion.
Detailed Research Plan: Plans PPI involvement across all three workstreams, from planning to co-design and refinement, ensuring inclusivity throughout the lifecycle.
Detailed Research Plan: States that compensation for time and expenses for the stakeholder group and focus group participants are costed in the budget.
Application Context: Specifies an 18-month duration and total cost of £180,401.32, providing concrete feasibility indicators for the project scope.
Application Context: States the total cost to NIHR as £180,401.32, establishing the funding level for resource justification.
Application Context: Identifies the contracting organisation as an Integrated Care Board, indicating the institutional setting for the award.

Plain English NLP Analysis: Flesch-Kincaid grade 5.3 and reading ease 74.35 indicate high readability; low jargon density (2.41%) and coverage of key elements (problem, objectives, methods, beneficiaries, impact) strongl
